title: The Learned Workman.
content:
Ne'er does he taste the fruit of the tree that he raised with such trouble;
    Nothing but taste e'er enjoys that which by learning is reared.
meaning:
"The Learned Workman." is a quintessential example of Friedrich Schiller's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
